description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just received two small CSV-style exports that live in the following directory:

/home/user/cloud_usage/raw_usage.csv  
/home/user/cloud_usage/pricing_adjustments.csv  

1. raw_usage.csv (exact contents, including header):
Service,ResourceID,UsageHours,CostUSD,Region
Compute,i-031,120,48.00,us-east-1
Storage,vol-22,720,36.00,us-east-1
Database,db-67,100,120.00,eu-west-1
Compute,i-099,50,20.00,us-east-1
Analytics,athena-1,30,15.00,us-west-2

2. pricing_adjustments.csv (exact contents, including header):
Service,DiscountPercent
Compute,10
Storage,5
Database,15
Analytics,20

The head of FinOps wants two new artifacts built entirely with standard shell tools such as cut, paste, awk, sort, etc.  No spreadsheets or Python allowed.

A. /home/user/cloud_usage/discounted_usage.csv  
   • Must contain exactly 6 comma-separated columns in the order below:  
     1) ResourceID  
     2) Service  
     3) Region  
     4) UsageHours  
     5) OriginalCostUSD  
     6) DiscountPercent  
     7) DiscountedCostUSD  
   • The first line is a header row written exactly as:  
     ResourceID,Service,Region,UsageHours,OriginalCostUSD,DiscountPercent,DiscountedCostUSD  
   • Follow with one data line for every line in raw_usage.csv (5 data lines total).  
   • The order of those 5 data lines must match the original order found in raw_usage.csv.  
   • Numerical columns 5, 6 and 7 must use two decimal places (e.g. 34.20).  
   • DiscountedCostUSD is calculated as: OriginalCostUSD × (1 − DiscountPercent / 100).

B. /home/user/cloud_usage/summary_by_service.csv  
   • Must contain exactly 4 comma-separated columns in the order below:  
     1) Service  
     2) TotalOriginalCostUSD  
     3) TotalDiscountedCostUSD  
     4) SavingsUSD  
   • The first line is a header row written exactly as:  
     Service,TotalOriginalCostUSD,TotalDiscountedCostUSD,SavingsUSD  
   • Follow with one aggregated line per unique service (4 data lines total).  
   • Lines must be sorted alphabetically by the Service column (A→Z).  
   • All numeric values must have two decimal places.  
   • SavingsUSD = TotalOriginalCostUSD − TotalDiscountedCostUSD.

C. /home/user/cloud_usage/optimization.log  
   • A plain text log that captures, one per line, the high-level shell command or pipeline you executed (do not include shell prompts or output).  
   • Each log line must be prefixed with a timestamp in ISO-8601 format accurate to seconds (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS).  
   • The file must contain at least three lines.

Hints (do NOT reveal actual commands):  
• You will need to join data from the two CSV files on the Service column.  
• Consider extracting columns with cut, using paste or join to combine, then calculating values with awk.  
• Remember to keep the header rows correct.  

Complete the task entirely from the Linux terminal.  When finished, all three files must exist with the exact formats described above so automated tests can pass.
truth:
Expected exact file contents after successful completion:

File: /home/user/cloud_usage/discounted_usage.csv
-------------------------------------------------
ResourceID,Service,Region,UsageHours,OriginalCostUSD,DiscountPercent,DiscountedCostUSD
i-031,Compute,us-east-1,120,48.00,10.00,43.20
vol-22,Storage,us-east-1,720,36.00,5.00,34.20
db-67,Database,eu-west-1,100,120.00,15.00,102.00
i-099,Compute,us-east-1,50,20.00,10.00,18.00
athena-1,Analytics,us-west-2,30,15.00,20.00,12.00

File: /home/user/cloud_usage/summary_by_service.csv
---------------------------------------------------
Service,TotalOriginalCostUSD,TotalDiscountedCostUSD,SavingsUSD
Analytics,15.00,12.00,3.00
Compute,68.00,61.20,6.80
Database,120.00,102.00,18.00
Storage,36.00,34.20,1.80

File: /home/user/cloud_usage/optimization.log  
---------------------------------------------------
• Must exist.  
• Must contain ≥3 lines.  
• Each line must begin with the pattern: 20??-??-??T??:??:?? (ISO-8601).  
• Content after the timestamp is free-form shell description; not validated for exact wording.

Pre-existing environment before agent starts:

Directory /home/user/cloud_usage is already present and contains the two source CSV files verbatim as listed in the public task description.  No other files are present.

Permissions:

The /home/user/cloud_usage directory is owned by user “user” and writable, so the agent can create new files there without sudo.
